items.title: Oil prices fall after Trump says U.S.-Iran talks in Qatar are going well
items.description: Oil prices were lower on Wednesday after Iran said it would not meet with U.S. delegates for talks in Qatar, amplifying concern about the peace process.

items.title: PlayStation will end physical disc production for new games in 2028
items.description: PlayStation will end physical disc production for new games in 2028, the company announced Wednesday morning.

items.title: Private payrolls rose by 98,000 in June, less than expected, ADP reports
items.description: Companies added slightly fewer workers than forecast, with hiring targeted heavily toward healthcare-related sectors.

items.title: Tech leads first half stock gains — but the biggest winners weren't in the U.S.
items.description: U.S. Big Tech stocks enjoyed strong gains — despite a sharp sell-off at the end of June — but were largely outperformed by their international counterparts.

items.title: South Korean government discriminated against Coupang, U.S. companies, House report finds
items.description: The House Judiciary Committee said the South Korean government discriminated against Coupang and other U.S. companies, in a new report.

items.title: U.S. auto industry faces more uncertainty without extension of USMCA trade deal
items.description: A major issue for automakers is the deal's rules of origin, which determine where a product comes from and which goods are eligible for preferential treatment.

items.title: Gold losses ease after worst quarter in 13 years as interest rate fears hit bullion
items.description: Gold fell further on Wednesday, with bullion continuing its slide after suffering its worst quarter since 2013.

items.title: Employers who laid off workers citing AI are already starting to regret it
items.description: Companies are realizing artificial intelligence can't do everything after all, prompting them to rehire employees to grow their businesses

items.title: We're exiting a troubled stock after a muted quarter, while considering better options
items.description: The quarter offered some signs that management's turnaround strategy is gaining traction, but progress remains slow.

items.title: Demand for riskier mortgages drops, as their advantages shrink
items.description: The spread between the 30-year fixed-rate mortgage and adjustable-rate loans is narrowing, causing demand for ARMs to weaken.

items.title: Trump Accounts for kids launch July 4: What parents need to know
items.description: Here's a breakdown of how Trump Accounts work, who qualifies for free money and when the funds can be withdrawn.

items.title: More than one-third of employees still work from home, new research shows
items.description: Despite return-to-office policies, the share of employees working from home increased in 2025 from the year before, according to the latest BLS data.

items.title: Buy the UK DIP? Defense stocks lifted by $20 billion spending boost as gilts come under fire
items.description: British defense stocks are marching higher once again after the U.K. confirmed a near-$20 billion military spending boost on Tuesday.

items.title: Bulls are betting big on this global ETF that's deep in a bear market
items.description: U.S. stocks just keep winning, with the Nasdaq closing out its best quarter since 2020. Across the globe in China, things couldn't be more different.

items.title: Trump's annual financial disclosure shows more than $580M in crypto-related income
items.description: Trump's 927-page financial disclosure report for 2025 totals more than 900 pages detailing holdings in cryptocurrency and stocks.

items.title: Anthropic says Trump admin has lifted export controls on Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5
items.description: The U.S. Department of Commerce lifted the export controls on Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models

items.title: Lamborghini reveals new Urus performance hybrid SUV after ditching EVs
items.description: Lamborghini is calling the new Urus SE Performante the "fastest Super SUV in the world."

items.title: Iran says it is selling oil at 20% premium as end of U.S. blockade sees 40 million barrels exported
items.description: The ceasefire prompted a surge in crude shipments through the Strait of Hormuz, where traffic had largely ground to a halt during the conflict.

items.title: Japan spent $74 billion propping up the yen. Investors say the real battle is with the Fed
items.description: Yen intervention alone is unlikely to reverse losses while wide U.S.-Japan rate differentials keep supporting the dollar, said analysts.

items.title: Why Asia still struggles to create its own mega-IPOs like SpaceX
items.description: Asia has no shortage of entrepreneurs, engineers or giant domestic markets. Yet when it comes to producing the kind of blockbuster listings seen in the U.S., the region continues to lag.
